info.description: In-DCS DCS Studio JSON-RPC bridge for the gui environment.
info.title: dcs-studio-gui

methods.description: Lines printed in the GUI state since sequence `after` (0/absent = from the start), as { lines = { { seq, text }, ... }, latest }. The mission bridge has its own ring on port 25570.
methods.name: console_read

methods.description: The real categories inside db.Units (Planes, Helicopters, Ships, Cars, …), shape-detected and filtered (GT_t/Skills and non-unit children are skipped). GUI bridge only; needs DCS loaded.
methods.name: db_categories

methods.description: Write pretty JSON to <writedir>Temp\dcs-studio-db-*.json and return { path, bytes } — a file, not a response payload, so a tens-of-MB dump never rides the WebSocket. `what` = all (default) | weapons | category:<name> | unit:<type>. Runs on the sim thread; `all` may stall for seconds (the 30s server timeout is the backstop).
methods.name: db_export
methods.summary: Dump part (or all) of the DCS database to a JSON file.

methods.description: Curated: { unit = { type, display_name, category, attributes, country_of_origin, crew_members, perf, guns, pylons } } where pylons carry per-store CLSIDs resolved against db.Weapons. `raw = true` returns the whole record deep-copied through a depth-capped, cycle-safe sanitizer. NB: ME loadout presets are NOT in db — 'payloads' here means pylons + compatible stores.
methods.name: db_unit

params.description: Return the whole record (sanitized) instead of the curated view.
params.name: raw

methods.summary: One unit record: curated summary, or the raw record.

methods.description: Light listing across one or all categories: { units = { { type, display_name, category }, ... }, truncated }. `filter` is a case-insensitive substring over type/display name; capped at 2000 rows.
methods.name: db_unit_types

params.description: Restrict to one category (name from db_categories).
params.name: category

methods.summary: List unit types (optionally one category, optionally filtered).

methods.description: Light listing of db.Weapons.ByCLSID: { weapons = { { clsid, display_name, name, category }, ... }, truncated }. `filter` is a case-insensitive substring over display name/name/CLSID; capped at 2000 rows.
methods.name: db_weapons

result.description: { weapons = { { clsid, display_name, name, category }, ... }, truncated }
result.name: weapons

methods.summary: List weapons/stores from db.Weapons (CLSID + display name).

methods.description: Drop every breakpoint and condition held by this bridge.
methods.name: debug_clear_breakpoints

methods.description: Resume a paused session: mode continue | step_over | step_into | step_out.
methods.name: debug_continue

methods.description: Evaluate an expression in a paused frame (locals → upvalues → globals). A top-level `name = value` assigns for real.
methods.name: debug_eval

methods.description: Lazily expand a variables/scope ref from the pause snapshot or the inspector.
methods.name: debug_expand

methods.description: Break at the next line of debugged code (manual pause).
methods.name: debug_pause

methods.description: Run a chunk under the debugger in the GUI state. Blocks for the whole session (the engine answers this bridge's RPCs itself while running/paused); poll debug_state instead of awaiting this call.
methods.name: debug_run

params.description: Chunkname; "=<abs path>" lines breakpoints up with the IDE.
params.name: source

params.description: Must be gui here; mission → port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Replace one source's breakpoints (+ per-line conditions) for GUI sessions: { source, breakpoints = { { line, condition? }, ... } }.
methods.name: debug_set_breakpoints

methods.description: Poll the session: { paused, running, snapshot?, error? }. Also the liveness signal that keeps a held pause alive.
methods.name: debug_state

methods.description: Terminate the running chunk (unwinds a runaway/looping run).
methods.name: debug_stop

methods.description: Introspect the live GUI-state API in _G (DCS, Export, net, lfs, log) as dotted .d.lua statements.
methods.name: dump_globals

methods.description: The generated EmmyLua (.d.lua) type definitions for this bridge's own Lua surface.
methods.name: emit_dlua

methods.description: Run Lua in the GUI/hooks state (DCS.*, net.*) and return the result. print() output streams into console_read. For the mission state use the mission bridge on port 25570.
methods.name: eval

methods.description: Re-dispatch the mission-bridge boot into the mission scripting state (fire-and-forget; needs a running mission and a desanitized MissionScripting.lua). Success = port 25570 answering; failures land in dcs.log.
methods.name: mission_boot

methods.description: Liveness check. dcs_time is mission model time (0 at the main menu).
methods.name: ping

methods.description: Drop every explorer ref held by the chosen environment.
methods.name: repl_clear

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Console eval in the chosen environment: { ok, result?, err? }.
methods.name: repl_eval

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Expand a ref handed out by repl_inspect/repl_expand: { ok, variables }.
methods.name: repl_expand

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Write the full JSON of a value (by ref or expression) to a file under <writedir>Temp\ and return { path, bytes }.
methods.name: repl_export

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Evaluate an expression and register the result for lazy drill-down: { ok, type, value, ref }.
methods.name: repl_inspect

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: Resolve a function ref's real parameter names (never runs the function): { ok, params?, native?, err? }.
methods.name: repl_signature

params.description: gui (default) | server | config | export. mission → use port 25570.
params.name: env

methods.description: The OpenRPC service description for this bridge — every JSON-RPC method it serves, with parameters and results. Per the OpenRPC spec, rpc.discover returns the service's OpenRPC document.
methods.name: rpc.discover

result.description: The OpenRPC document describing this bridge.
result.name: OpenRPC Schema
